note: X25519 known-answer test (KAT) vectors for the WireGuard key-derivation definition the conformance harness pins. public_b64 is the base64.StdEncoding form of X25519(private, basepoint) = the WireGuard public key, derived stdlib-side via crypto/ecdh X25519 — the SAME definition internal/localcompile.ecdhKeygen.DerivePublic wraps (proven byte-equal to the default wgtypesKeygen over 10k inputs in keygen_equivalence_test.go). plan-4 asserts @noble/curves against THIS file, so plans 3/4/5 all pin one X25519 derivation. keygen_kat_test.go is the Go anchor.

vectors.name: rfc7748_6.1_alice
vectors.doc: RFC 7748 section 6.1 Alice's private key -> her public key (scalar * basepoint). Authoritative IETF test vector.

vectors.name: rfc7748_6.1_bob
vectors.doc: RFC 7748 section 6.1 Bob's private key -> his public key. Authoritative IETF test vector.

vectors.name: clamp_low_byte_all_ones
vectors.doc: private key = 32 bytes of 0x01. Exercises the low-byte clamp (key[0] &= 248 clears the 3 low bits). crypto/ecdh clamps internally during scalar mult, so the UNCLAMPED 0x01.. private derives the same public key as its clamped form (asserted in clamp_equivalence).

vectors.name: clamp_high_byte_all_ff
vectors.doc: private key = 32 bytes of 0xff. Exercises the high-byte clamp (key[31] &= 127 clears the top bit; key[31] |= 64 sets bit 6). The unclamped 0xff.. private derives the same public key as its clamped form.

vectors.name: clamp_all_zero
vectors.doc: private key = 32 zero bytes. After clamping, bit 6 of the last byte is set (key[31] |= 64), so this is NOT the identity scalar — it derives a well-defined non-zero public key. Boundary case for key[31] |= 64.

clamp_equivalence.name: all_ones_raw_vs_clamped
clamp_equivalence.doc: A raw private key and its X25519-clamped form (key[0] &= 248; key[31] &= 127; key[31] |= 64) MUST derive the IDENTICAL public key, because X25519 clamps the scalar internally. This is the property a TS @noble port must reproduce: do NOT pre-clamp before deriving, or do so identically.

clamp_equivalence.name: all_ff_raw_vs_clamped
clamp_equivalence.doc: Same equivalence over the high-byte clamp boundary: 0xff.. and its clamped form 0xf8..0x7f derive the identical public key.

clamp_equivalence.name: all_zero_raw_vs_clamped
clamp_equivalence.doc: Zero scalar vs its clamped form (only bit 6 of the last byte set): identical public keys.
